invariants.invariant: No regex — use ast.parse(), yaml.safe_load(), str.split()
Loud failures — raise on mismatch, never silently pass
No hardcoded params — hashes from config, thresholds from YAML
Config changes authorized by security assessment doc

execution-order.note: T1 and T2 both modify Makefile — T2 runs after T1 to avoid conflicts. T3 and T4 touch independent files, can run with T1.

task.title: Replace Trivy with Grype for Docker image vulnerability scanning
task.description:
Replace Trivy references with Grype for DOCKER IMAGE scanning only. Grype already exists in Makefile for Python SBOM scanning (sbom-scan target). This task extends Grype to cover Docker images and removes Trivy entirely.
REVIEWER CORRECTION R1: Grype already has sbom-scan target (line 360). Only the Docker image scan targets (scan:, sbom:, install-trivy:) need replacing.
REVIEWER CORRECTION R2: trivy-critical Makefile target does NOT exist but is called by pr_readiness_check.sh (line 39). This is a pre-existing bug. Create grype-critical target to fix it.
REVIEWER CORRECTION R9: T2 also modifies Makefile — T2 runs after T1.

tdd-approach.red: Read ALL 5 files first (Rule #11). Update tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_security_tooling.py: - Rename Trivy assertions to Grype equivalents - Add test_makefile_has_install_grype_target - Add test_makefile_has_grype_critical_target (fixes pre-existing bug) - Add test_weekly_scan_uses_grype (no "trivy" in executable lines) - Add test_grype_version_pinned (version string in install command) - Add test_pr_readiness_calls_grype_critical Keep test_makefile_has_scan_target (scan: target name unchanged) Run: expect RED
tdd-approach.green:
Makefile: - Replace install-trivy → install-grype (pinned v0.90.0) - Replace scan: to use grype docker: instead of trivy image - Add grype-critical: target (--fail-on critical) - Keep sbom-scan: as-is (already uses Grype) - Update .PHONY
scripts/weekly_security_scan.sh: trivy image → grype docker: scripts/pr_readiness_check.sh: make trivy-critical → make grype-critical deployment/CLAUDE.md: update scanner references

task.title: Add pip-audit pre-commit hook for CVE scanning
task.description:
Add pip-audit as a local pre-commit hook. Scans installed packages against the OSV database for known CVEs. Runs locally (no CI credits).
REVIEWER CORRECTION R3: pip-audit must be added to pyproject.toml dev dependencies FIRST. Use language: system. Gate on dependency files (pyproject.toml, uv.lock) to avoid running on every commit.
REVIEWER CORRECTION R9: Runs after T1 because both modify Makefile.

tdd-approach.red: Read .pre-commit-config.yaml and pyproject.toml first. Create tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_pip_audit_hook.py: - test_pre_commit_has_pip_audit_hook (yaml.safe_load, find hook id) - test_pip_audit_hook_is_local_system (language: system) - test_pip_audit_hook_uses_uv_run ("uv run pip-audit" in entry) - test_pip_audit_hook_uses_strict ("--strict" in entry) - test_pip_audit_hook_gated_on_dep_files (files: pattern includes pyproject.toml) - test_pip_audit_in_dev_dependencies (tomllib parse pyproject.toml) - test_makefile_has_audit_target ("audit:" in Makefile) Run: expect RED
tdd-approach.green: 1. Add "pip-audit>=2.7" to pyproject.toml dev dependencies 2. Run: uv sync --all-extras 3. Add to .pre-commit-config.yaml (after existing local hooks): - repo: local hooks: - id: pip-audit name: pip-audit CVE scan entry: uv run pip-audit --strict --desc language: system pass_filenames: false always_run: false files: '(pyproject\.toml|uv\.lock)$' 4. Add audit: target to Makefile
tdd-approach.verify: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_pip_audit_hook.py -v uv run pip-audit --strict --desc 2>&1 | tail -5 make test-staging

task.title: SHA-256 hash pinning for HuggingFace model weight downloads
task.description:
Add hash verification for downloaded model weights. Two distinct mechanisms:
REVIEWER CORRECTION R4: SAM3 uses from_pretrained() which downloads MULTIPLE sharded safetensors files via HuggingFace Transformers API. Hash pinning a single file does NOT work for SAM3. Instead, pin the HF model revision (commit SHA) which guarantees content integrity.
VesselFM uses hf_hub_download() for a SINGLE file — SHA-256 pinning works directly here.
REVIEWER CORRECTION R5: VesselFM adapter has VESSELFM_WEIGHT_SHA256 = None at src/minivess/adapters/vesselfm.py:37. This DISABLES verification. The verify_checksum() call at line 56 always returns True when hash is None. This is the most urgent fix.
REVIEWER CORRECTION R10: Hash verification belongs in adapter code (already partially implemented), NOT in SkyPilot setup block (Rule #31).
Strategy: - SAM3: Pin HF model revision (commit SHA) in model profile YAML - VesselFM: Populate VESSELFM_WEIGHT_SHA256 with actual hash - Reuse existing checkpoint_integrity.py compute_checkpoint_sha256()
task.rule31-authorizations: Adding weights.hf_revision to model profile YAMLs — authorized by security assessment. Populating VESSELFM_WEIGHT_SHA256 — authorized (fixing a disabled security check).

tdd-approach.green:
1. Create scripts/verify_model_weights.py: - verify_model_weight_sha256(path, expected_hash) using compute_checkpoint_sha256() from checkpoint_integrity - CLI entry point for manual verification
2. Fix src/minivess/adapters/vesselfm.py: - Download VesselFM weight once locally - Compute: python -c "from minivess.pipeline.checkpoint_integrity import compute_checkpoint_sha256; from pathlib import Path; print(compute_checkpoint_sha256(Path('~/.cache/huggingface/.../vesselFM_base.pt').expanduser()))" - Set VESSELFM_WEIGHT_SHA256 to the computed value - If weight file not available locally, set to a placeholder string "POPULATE_AFTER_FIRST_DOWNLOAD" and add test that flags this
3. Update model profile YAMLs — add weights section: weights: hf_repo: "facebook/sam3-hiera-large" hf_revision: null # Pin after first verified download For SAM3: hf_revision pins the model commit SHA (not file hash) For VesselFM: weights.sha256 pins the file hash
4. Do NOT modify SkyPilot YAML setup block (Rule #31, R10). Hash verification happens in adapter load_checkpoint() code.

task.title: Audit torch.load(weights_only=False) — convert where safe, guard the rest
task.description:
Audit all 9 occurrences of weights_only=False across 6 source files. Convert to weights_only=True where safe. Add AST guard test with file/function allowlist for justified exceptions.
REVIEWER CORRECTION R6: 9 call sites across 6 files, not 6: - checkpoint_utils.py: 2 (load_checkpoint_with_fallback primary + fallback) - checkpoint_averaging.py: 1 - model_merging.py: 3 - subsampled_ensemble.py: 1 - pipeline/post_training_plugins/swag.py: 1 - ensemble/swag.py: 1
REVIEWER CORRECTION R7: Use file:function allowlist, not bare count. Each allowed exception must have a # SECURITY comment.
REVIEWER CORRECTION R8: Also scan for pickle.load() and pickle.loads() in production code (equally dangerous).
NOTE: All 9 calls load SELF-PRODUCED checkpoints (not external weights). The adapter files (src/minivess/adapters/) all already use weights_only=True.

tdd-approach.red:
Read ALL 6 source files first (Rule #11). Understand what each torch.load call loads and what types are in the checkpoint dicts.
Create tests/v2/unit/pipeline/test_torch_load_safety.py:
ALLOWLIST = { # file_stem: [function_names] — justified weights_only=False # Each entry must have a # SECURITY comment in source }
class TestTorchLoadWeightsOnlyAudit: def test_weights_only_false_count_within_allowlist(self): """AST scan src/minivess/ for weights_only=False calls. Assert each is in the ALLOWLIST and has # SECURITY comment.""" Use ast.parse() + ast.walk() on all .py files under src/minivess/. Find ast.keyword nodes with arg="weights_only" and value=ast.Constant(value=False). For each: check file + enclosing function is in ALLOWLIST. Fail on any unallowlisted occurrence.
def test_no_weights_only_false_in_adapters(self): """Adapters load external weights — must use weights_only=True.""" AST scan src/minivess/adapters/ only. Assert ZERO weights_only=False calls.
def test_no_bare_pickle_load_in_production(self): """pickle.load() and pickle.loads() banned in src/.""" AST scan for ast.Call where func matches pickle.load/loads. Assert count == 0. (Currently clean — codify the invariant.)
def test_all_weights_only_false_have_security_comment(self): """Each weights_only=False line must have # SECURITY on same or preceding line.""" For each file with allowed exceptions, read source lines. Find line numbers with weights_only=False. Assert nearby line contains "SECURITY".
class TestCheckpointRoundTrip: def test_standard_checkpoint_loads_with_weights_only_true(self): """Self-produced checkpoint (state_dict + epoch + optimizer) can be loaded with weights_only=True if we add safe_globals.""" Create a checkpoint dict with Tensor values, int epoch, float loss. torch.save to tmp_path. torch.load with weights_only=True. Assert all values match.
Run: expect RED (no SECURITY comments yet, allowlist empty)
tdd-approach.green:
For each of the 9 call sites:
1. TRY converting to weights_only=True: - If the checkpoint only contains standard types (Tensor, dict, list, int, float, str): convert directly. - If it contains OrderedDict: add torch.serialization.add_safe_globals()
2. If conversion FAILS (e.g., checkpoint contains nn.Module instances): - Keep weights_only=False - Add comment: # SECURITY: weights_only=False required — loads [specific reason]. Self-produced checkpoints only.
3. Add each KEPT exception to ALLOWLIST in the test.
4. Expected outcome: - checkpoint_utils.py: likely CONVERT (loads state_dicts) - checkpoint_averaging.py: likely CONVERT - model_merging.py: likely CONVERT (3 calls) - subsampled_ensemble.py: likely CONVERT - swag.py (plugin): KEEP (may load full model object) - ensemble/swag.py: TRY CONVERT (nested dicts of tensors)
tdd-approach.verify: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/pipeline/test_torch_load_safety.py -v uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/pipeline/ -v (all pipeline tests) make test-staging

final-gate.command: uv run ruff check src/ tests/
